preview_text:
The model favors Panama to win at 66%, while Ghana's chances sit at 11%. This represents a substantial gap: Panama is the clear statistical favorite, though Ghana retains a meaningful, if modest, path to victory.

The draw emerges as the second-most-likely outcome in the model at 24%, suggesting the match will not necessarily be decided by a wide margin. The 55-percentage-point spread between Panama's win probability and Ghana's reflects a competitive imbalance, but the presence of a near-quarter-chance at a draw indicates the teams may compete closely in regulation time.

The market consensus diverges sharply from the model. Sportsbooks price Ghana's win probability at 46%—35 percentage points higher than the model—and Panama at only 27%, a 39-point gap in the opposite direction. This represents a fundamental disagreement about relative strength. Polymarket pricing shows a uniform split across all three outcomes, reflecting maximum uncertainty. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the large discrepancy warrants close attention to how consensus evolves.
